name: Build-a-Company Hack Test
description: Hackathon test template for company structures with teams, leaders, cross-team handoffs, and reusable workflow contracts.
author: ClawMax Team

agents.name: CEO
agents.role: Sets company direction, priorities, and final decision-making.

agents.name: Execution Lead
agents.role: Turns company goals into an execution plan, milestones, and operating briefs.

agents.name: Program Manager
agents.role: Keeps milestones, dependencies, and execution follow-through aligned across teams.

agents.name: Engineering Lead
agents.role: Owns technical planning, implementation sequencing, and engineering coordination.

agents.name: Platform Engineer
agents.role: Breaks the execution plan into implementation milestones, dependencies, and delivery notes.

agents.name: Marketing Lead
agents.role: Owns launch messaging, content packaging, and market-facing execution.

agents.name: Content Strategist
agents.role: Turns launch goals into messaging, campaign assets, and reusable content packages.

agents.name: QA Lead
agents.role: Validates quality, release readiness, and cross-team handoff completeness.

agents.name: Release Analyst
agents.role: Checks handoff completeness, release risks, and readiness evidence across teams.

teams.name: Build-a-Company Hackathon Org
teams.purpose: Root company team for the hackathon demo organization.

teams.name: Leadership
teams.purpose: Set direction and issue the initial company brief.

teams.name: Execution
teams.purpose: Translate leadership direction into an execution brief with milestones and owners.

teams.name: Engineering
teams.purpose: Convert the execution brief into a technical plan and delivery document.

teams.name: Marketing
teams.purpose: Turn the execution and engineering outputs into launch messaging and campaign assets.

teams.name: QA
teams.purpose: Validate the end-to-end package, confirm readiness, and produce a release review.

communities.name: Build-a-Company
communities.description: Cross-company coordination space for the hackathon template.

groups.name: Marketing
groups.description: Marketing launch planning and content packaging

workflows.name: Leadership Kickoff
workflows.description: Create the company brief and strategic direction.
workflows.content:
# Leadership Kickoff

Produce the company direction and leadership brief.

## Success Criteria
- Keep the output under 150 words.
- Use 4 bullets max.
- State goals, top priority, top risk, and the next handoff.

## Output
- Publish a short leadership brief for downstream teams.

workflows.name: Execution Brief
workflows.description: Turn the leadership brief into an execution plan with milestones and owners.
workflows.content:
# Execution Brief

Use the upstream leadership brief to produce an execution plan.

## Success Criteria
- Keep the output under 180 words.
- Use 5 bullets max.
- Include owner, milestone, dependency, and handoff target.

## Output
- Publish a short execution plan for engineering and marketing.

workflows.name: Engineering Plan
workflows.description: Turn the execution plan into a delivery plan owned by engineering.
workflows.content:
# Engineering Plan

Use the upstream execution plan to produce the engineering spec.

## Success Criteria
- Keep the output under 180 words.
- Use 5 bullets max.
- Include scope, dependency, sequencing, and release note for QA.

## Output
- Publish a short engineering spec for downstream teams.

workflows.name: Marketing Launch
workflows.description: Turn the execution plan and engineering spec into launch messaging and campaign assets.
workflows.content:
# Marketing Launch

Use the upstream execution plan and engineering spec to produce launch messaging.

## Success Criteria
- Keep the output under 180 words.
- Use 5 bullets max.
- Include positioning, one headline, one CTA, and rollout note.

## Output
- Publish a short marketing pack with messaging and rollout copy.

workflows.name: QA Review
workflows.description: Validate the combined package and produce a release readiness review.
workflows.content:
# QA Review

Use the downstream engineering and marketing outputs to validate release readiness.

## Success Criteria
- Keep the output under 150 words.
- Use 4 bullets max.
- Include readiness status, top blocker, top risk, and next action.

## Output
- Publish a short QA signoff with risks, blockers, and next action.
